scheme_name: Subsidy on Rehri alongwith (Stove, Gas Chulha, Utensil etc.) for the Fish/Fish Product Sale
page_title: Subsidy on Rehri alongwith (Stove, Gas Chulha, Utensil etc.) for the Fish/Fish Product Sale

details: The scheme “Subsidy on Rehri along with Stove, Gas Chulha, Utensils, etc. for Sale of Fish/Fish Products” is implemented by the Fisheries Department, Government of Haryana. Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to Scheduled Caste fish farmers for the purchase of a Rehri (handcart) equipped with stove, gas chulha, utensils, and other essentials for selling fish and fish products. A 60% subsidy is provided on the total project cost of ₹60,000/- per beneficiary.
benefits: Under this scheme, financial assistance will be provided for the purchase of a Rehri (Handcart) along with essential items such as stove, gas chulha, utensils, etc., for selling fish or fish products.
A subsidy of 60% on the total project cost of ₹60,000/- will be granted, amounting to ₹36,000/- per beneficiary.
eligibility: The applicant should have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
The applicant's age should be a minimum of 18 years.

online: For registration, click on “New User/Register Here” and fill in all the mandatory details i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number & password. Click on ‘Submit’.
Verify the OTP received in the provided Email and Mobile Number. Alternatively, click on the Verification Link to activate the profile.
Login to Apply for the Scheme:
The “Sign in here” option is available on the right side of the screen. Fill in the required credentials and click on the ‘Login’ button.
In the opened window, click on “Scheme/Services list”, and a list of schemes will appear on the screen.
Now, select the scheme and click on “Apply for Service/Scheme”.
Fill in all the details in the online application form and click on "Submit".
Type SARAL and send to 9954699899 to track your application.
Type SARAL<space><Application ID/Ticket No.> and send to 9954699899 to track your application.

documents_required: Agreement Deed (Agreement deed between the fish farmer and the Fisheries Department)
Date of Birth Certificate (Birth Certificate/Matriculation Certificate/PAN Card/Voter Card/Driving License)
Identity Proof (Ration Card/Aadhar Card/PAN Card/Voter Card)
Caste Certificate (Caste Certificate issued by 1st Class Magistrate)
Training Certificate (Training from any Government Institute)

faqs.question: What is the primary purpose of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The primary purpose of the scheme is to provide subsidy for the purchase of Rehri, along with equipment like stoves, gas chulhas, and utensils, for the sale of fish and fish products.

faqs.question: Who are the specific target beneficiaries for this scheme, as mentioned in the eligibility criteria?
faqs.answer: The scheme is specifically targeted towards Schedule Caste families for their welfare in the fisheries sector.

faqs.question: What percentage of the total project cost is provided as a subsidy under this scheme?
faqs.answer: A 60% subsidy is provided on the total project cost for the purchase of the Rehri and associated items.

faqs.question: Is it a mandatory requirement for the applicant to possess a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) for scheme eligibility?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant must have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) to be eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: Does the applicant need to be a permanent resident of Haryana to qualify for this welfare scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant must be a permanent resident of Haryana to be eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: Which specific category of citizens is this scheme designed to benefit, based on the provided information?
faqs.answer: The scheme is designed to benefit applicants who belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.

faqs.question: What is the minimum age an applicant must have attained to be considered eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant must have attained a minimum age of 18 years to be considered eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: Which documents are accepted as valid proof of the applicant's date of birth for the application?
faqs.answer: Birth Certificate, Matriculation Certificate, PAN Card, Voter Card, or Driving License are accepted as valid proof of date of birth.

faqs.question: What are the acceptable forms of identity proof that an applicant can submit with their application?
faqs.answer: Ration Card, Aadhar Card, PAN Card, or Voter Card are accepted as valid forms of identity proof.

faqs.question: What is the fee charged by the government for applying for the scheme?
faqs.answer: There is no government fee charged for applying for this subsidy scheme.
